pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 10 defines PROJECT_FILE=$DOCS_DIR/bone_analysis.inv3. export_result.sh line 12 confirms path.
setup_task.sh line 11 defines REPORT_FILE=$DOCS_DIR/bone_report.txt. export_result.sh line 13 confirms path.

Verifier.py lines 85-88 contain code comment: 'Check Compact Bone (Adult) ~ 662 to 1988'. create_tissue_atlas_project/task.json metadata lines 20-22 specify Dense_Bone min_hu: 662. boolean_mask_operations/validated_pi.json line 31 confirms compact_bone_hu_min: 662 HU verified from PubMed research. Multi-tissue tasks consistently reference 662 HU minimum threshold for cortical/compact bone segmentation.

Verifier.py lines 85-88 comment states '~ 662 to 1988'. create_tissue_atlas_project/task.json metadata lines 20-22 specify Dense_Bone max_hu: 3071 (full range) but cortical bone upper is ~1988. boolean_mask_operations/validated_pi.json line 38 confirms compact_bone_hu_max: 1988 HU as standard cortical bone range. Medical imaging literature confirms adult compact bone extends to approximately 1988-2000 HU.

Verifier.py lines 90-93 contain code comment: 'Check Spongial Bone (Adult) ~ 148 to 661'. create_tissue_atlas_project/task.json metadata lines 25-28 specify Spongy_Bone min_hu: 148. Multiple verified InVesalius task definitions consistently document 148 HU as the standard minimum threshold for spongial/spongy bone segmentation.

Verifier.py lines 90-93 comment states '~ 148 to 661'. create_tissue_atlas_project/task.json metadata lines 25-28 specify Spongy_Bone max_hu: 661. This upper threshold precisely separates spongy bone from the transition to cortical bone density range, which begins at 662 HU.

Verifier.py line 114 enforces volume validation with condition 'vol_c > 10 and vol_s > 10'. This 10 mL threshold is reasonable for human bone segmentation from CT imaging. Value appears in verifier logic and is consistent with anatomical expectations for skull bone volumes.

pi_items.verified_value: Official InVesalius v3.0 CT Cranium sample dataset (file 0051.zip), real de-identified patient CT scan published in GitHub release April 2017. Downloaded from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip and symlinked to /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh references /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium which is symlinked to /opt/invesalius/sample_data/ct_cranium. evidence_docs/dicom_dir_listing.txt confirms this symlink. boolean_mask_operations/validated_pi.json and multi_tissue_surface_export/validated_pi.json both verify this is the official InVesalius sample dataset, not synthetic, from v3.0 release.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the official InVesalius 3.0 CT Cranium sample dataset (0051), a real de-identified medical imaging dataset. The task output files are correctly specified at /home/ga/Documents/bone_analysis.inv3 (project) and /home/ga/Documents/bone_report.txt (report). However, the Hounsfield unit thresholds in the metadata contain discrepancies: the claimed values (compact bone 600-1900 HU, spongial bone 140-700 HU) do not match the actual InVesalius preset thresholds documented in other tasks and the verifier's own comments (compact bone 662-1988 HU, spongial bone 148-661 HU). The minimum plausible bone volume of 10.0 mL is correctly specified and used in the verifier logic.
